questions.q: Explain the general methods for the preparation of alcohols. Include at least two methods and provide suitable examples. Also, mention the importance of alcohols in everyday life.
questions.answer:
Alcohols are prepared through various methods, including the hydration of alkynes, alkenes, and alkanes. For instance, the hydration of alkenes is a common method, where alkenes react with water in the presence of an acid catalyst to form alcohols. For example, the hydration of ethene yields ethanol.

Alcohols are essential in everyday life, serving as solvents, disinfectants, and fuels. They are also used as raw materials in the synthesis of various compounds, such as detergents, plastics, and pharmaceuticals.
marking_scheme: Mention at least two methods of alcohol preparation (1 mark)
Provide suitable examples for each method (1 mark)
Mention importance of alcohols in everyday life (1 mark)
Provide relevant details about each example (1 mark)
Mention the role of alcohols in the synthesis of various compounds (1 mark)

questions.q: Explain the reaction of alcohols with sodium. Mention the products formed and the conditions required for the reaction.
questions.answer:
Alcohols react with sodium to form sodium alkoxides and hydrogen gas. The reaction is a vigorous and exothermic process, requiring dry and purified sodium and anhydrous alcohol. For example, the reaction of ethanol with sodium yields sodium ethoxide and hydrogen gas.

2Na + 2Câ‚‚Hâ‚…OH â†’ 2Câ‚‚Hâ‚…ONa + Hâ‚‚
marking_scheme: Mention the products formed in the reaction (1 mark)
Describe the conditions required for the reaction (1 mark)
Provide a clear and accurate equation for the reaction (1 mark)
Mention the characteristics of the reaction (1 mark)
Explain the importance of the reaction (1 mark)

questions.q: Calculate the number of moles of ethanol (Câ‚‚Hâ‚…OH) required to produce 2.5 L of hydrogen gas at STP.
questions.answer:
Calculation:

2Câ‚‚Hâ‚…OH + 2Na â†’ 2Câ‚‚Hâ‚…ONa + Hâ‚‚
1 mole Câ‚‚Hâ‚…OH produces 1 mole Hâ‚‚

Given: Volume of Hâ‚‚ = 2.5 L
At STP, 1 mole of gas occupies 22.4 L

Moles of Hâ‚‚ = Volume of Hâ‚‚ / 22.4 L/mol
= 2.5 L / 22.4 L/mol
= 0.1118 mol

Since 1 mole Câ‚‚Hâ‚…OH produces 1 mole Hâ‚‚, the number of moles of Câ‚‚Hâ‚…OH required is also 0.1118 mol
marking_scheme: Derive the balanced chemical equation (1 mark)
Identify the mole ratio between Câ‚‚Hâ‚…OH and Hâ‚‚ (1 mark)
Calculate the number of moles of Hâ‚‚ produced (2 marks)
Determine the number of moles of Câ‚‚Hâ‚…OH required (1 mark)

questions.q: Calculate the mass of 2-methyl-2-propanol (CHâ‚ƒC(CHâ‚ƒ)â‚‚OH) required to produce 3.2 g of water.
questions.answer:
Calculation:

CHâ‚ƒC(CHâ‚ƒ)â‚‚OH + Hâ‚‚O â†’ CHâ‚ƒC(CHâ‚ƒ)â‚‚OHâ‚‚âپ؛ + OHâپ»

However, the actual reaction is:

CHâ‚ƒC(CHâ‚ƒ)â‚‚OH + Hâ‚‚O â†’ CHâ‚ƒC(CHâ‚ƒ)â‚‚OHâ‚‚âپ؛ + OHâپ»

But considering the actual reaction as:

2CHâ‚ƒC(CHâ‚ƒ)â‚‚OH + Hâ‚‚O â†’ (CHâ‚ƒC(CHâ‚ƒ)â‚‚OH)â‚‚O + Hâ‚‚O

2 moles CHâ‚ƒC(CHâ‚ƒ)â‚‚OH produce 1 mole Hâ‚‚O

Molar mass of CHâ‚ƒC(CHâ‚ƒ)â‚‚OH = 74.12 g/mol

Given: Mass of Hâ‚‚O = 3.2 g
Molar mass of Hâ‚‚O = 18.02 g/mol

Moles of Hâ‚‚O = Mass of Hâ‚‚O / Molar mass of Hâ‚‚O
= 3.2 g / 18.02 g/mol
= 0.1775 mol

Since 2 moles CHâ‚ƒC(CHâ‚ƒ)â‚‚OH produce 1 mole Hâ‚‚O, the number of moles of CHâ‚ƒC(CHâ‚ƒ)â‚‚OH required is 0.355 mol

Mass of CHâ‚ƒC(CHâ‚ƒ)â‚‚OH = Moles of CHâ‚ƒC(CHâ‚ƒ)â‚‚OH x Molar mass of CHâ‚ƒC(CHâ‚ƒ)â‚‚OH
= 0.355 mol x 74.12 g/mol
= 26.28 g

questions.q: Calculate the molarity of a 2 M solution of phenol (Câ‚†Hâ‚…OH) if 10 mL of the solution requires 22.5 mL of 0.5 M NaOH for titration.
questions.answer:
Calculation:

The reaction between phenol and NaOH is:

Câ‚†Hâ‚…OH + NaOH â†’ Câ‚†Hâ‚…ONa + Hâ‚‚O

The reaction is a 1:1 ratio between phenol and NaOH.

Given: Volume of NaOH required = 22.5 mL
Molarity of NaOH = 0.5 M

Number of moles of NaOH = Molarity of NaOH x Volume of NaOH (in L)
= 0.5 M x 0.0225 L
= 0.01125 mol

Since the reaction is a 1:1 ratio, the number of moles of phenol required is also 0.01125 mol

Molarity of phenol = Number of moles of phenol / Volume of phenol (in L)
= 0.01125 mol / 0.01 L
= 1.125 M
marking_scheme: Derive the balanced chemical equation (1 mark)
Identify the mole ratio between phenol and NaOH (1 mark)
Calculate the number of moles of NaOH required (2 marks)
Determine the number of moles of phenol required (1 mark)
Calculate the molarity of the phenol solution (1 mark)
questions.topic: Acidic and Basic Nature of Phenol
